description_long: This wall features a layered construction with an exterior of reddish-brown brickwork on one side and white brickwork on the other, separated by a visible horizontal band that may represent insulation or a structural break. The top surface is capped with a flat, dark brown slab, possibly concrete or stone, and a lighter central strip, suggesting a composite or insulated core. The brickwork is arranged in a running bond pattern, with the reddish-brown bricks forming a partial facade and the white bricks extending further along the length. The cross-section reveals a multi-layered build, including what appears to be a cavity or insulation layer between the brick faces. The wall is robust and solid, typical of urban boundary or retaining walls, and lacks any visible openings or decorative embellishments. The overall appearance is clean and well-maintained, with sharp edges and uniform brickwork.
description: A robust, multi-layered wall with reddish-brown and white brickwork, capped by a flat dark slab and a central light strip, showing a clean, well-maintained urban construction.
description_view_0: Side view highlighting the layered brick construction, with reddish-brown bricks in the foreground and a visible cross-section of the wall's internal structure.
description_view_1: Top-down view showing the flat brown slab and central light strip, with both brick colors visible along the sides.
description_view_2: Opposite side view emphasizing the white brickwork and the wall's thickness, with the brown slab and internal layers visible.
description_view_3: Rear view dominated by white brickwork, with partial reddish-brown brick accents and no visible openings or features.
